negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Long-running HTTP Phase 2 pricing ignores delivery_result_len and charges only FEE_PER_POLL_BLOCK_WEI * blocks_polled instead of polling_fee plus http_executor_fee(0, delivery_result_len).
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/ritual_async.rs and crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs pass delivery result length into calculate_async_fees_phase2.
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs branches LONG_RUNNING_HTTP_PRECOMPILE to AsyncFees::phase2_long_running(blocks_polled), whose executor fee is polling only.

notes: Confirmed gap: builder/verifier parity exists, but both share a helper that undercharges long-running HTTP delivery payload bytes.
rationale: The commit adds calculate_async_fees_phase2(), uses delivery_result_len in payload and verification, documents validator fees as already paid in Phase 1, and sets Phase 2 commitment/inclusion validator fees to zero. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Phase 2 long-running HTTP settlement fees must pay only the executor, with executor_fee equal to polling_fee plus http_executor_fee(0, delivery_result_len), and commitment and inclusion validator fees set to zero.
suggested_falsification_tests: For a 0x0805 job with known commit block, settlement block, and delivery_result length, assert payload construction and block verification compute the same executor fee as fee_per_poll_block * blocks_polled plus http_executor_fee(0, len), with zero validator fees.
summary: Long-running HTTP Phase 2 pricing omits the payload fee.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For a 0x0805 delivery with a non-empty delivery result, compare expected polling + http_executor_fee(0, len) against current calculate_async_fees_phase2 output; current output omits the payload fee.
title: Phase 2 pricing is polling plus payload
